title: Twin ‘super-puff’ planets offer clues to cosmic evolution - Financial Times
description: Celestial bodies more than 1,000 light years from Earth are less dense than candyfloss
factsCount: Twin “super-puff” planets are more than 1,000 light years from Earth.; The twin planets are less dense than candyfloss.; The planets offer clues to cosmic evolution.
topic: Should the discovery of low‑density “super‑puff” twin exoplanets fundamentally reshape scientific models of planetary formation and cosmic evolution?
background: Twin “super‑puff” planets have been identified more than 1,000 light‑years from Earth. Their measured densities are lower than that of candyfloss, making them some of the fluffiest known worlds. Researchers note that these planets could provide valuable clues about the processes that drive cosmic evolution.
framing_debate: Do these ultra‑low‑density exoplanets warrant a revision of current theories of how planets form and evolve in the universe?
